subject: Chemistry
title: Ibuprofen: The Anti-Inflammatory
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Ibuprofen: The Anti-Inflammatory.

hook.text: Stewart Adams spent 10 years developing ibuprofen. He first tested it on himself—for a hangover headache.

buildup.text: Adams at Boots UK wanted a safer alternative to aspirin. He screened over 600 compounds before finding ibuprofen in 1961.

discovery.text: Ibuprofen blocks COX enzymes that produce prostaglandins—chemicals that cause pain, fever, and inflammation.

twist.text: COX-2 inhibitors were designed to be even safer. But Vioxx caused heart attacks—and was pulled from the market in 2004.

climax.text: Ibuprofen is now sold in over 120 countries. It treats headaches, arthritis, and fever for billions of people worldwide.

punchline.text: 600 failed compounds, one hangover test. A decade of work became a household staple.

quiz.question: How does ibuprofen reduce pain and inflammation?
options: It blocks COX enzymes that produce prostaglandins causing pain and inflammation
It numbs nerve endings directly at the site of injury
It reduces blood flow to inflamed areas
